sections.title: 1. Introduction
sections.content: The Brake Controller unit implements the primary braking logic for the VCU Engine Firmware. It receives driver pedal inputs and wheel-speed sensor data, computes the optimal brake torque distribution, and issues commands to the individual wheel actuators.

sections.title: 2. Interfaces
sections.content:
| ID | Direction | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| IF_PedalPos | Input | float32 | Brake pedal position 0-100% |
| IF_WheelSpd | Input | float32[4] | Individual wheel speeds in rpm |
| IF_DiagStatus | Output | uint32 | Diagnostic trouble code bitmask |

sections.title: 3. Static Design
sections.content: The module is structured as a single cyclic runnable `BrakeCtrl_Run` executing at 10 ms. Internal state is maintained in a static struct `BrakeCtrl_State_t` holding slip-ratio accumulators and PID integrators.

sections.title: 4. Dynamic Design
sections.content: On each cycle: (1) read inputs, (2) compute slip ratios, (3) run PID controller, (4) clamp output to actuator limits, (5) write outputs and update diagnostics.
